# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'ARTIFACT_URL', defaultValue: 'https://example.com/artifacts/my-app-1.0.0.tar.gz', description: 'URL of the artifact to download (e.g., S3 pre-signed URL, Artifactory download link, HTTP/HTTPS URL)')
        string(name: 'ARTIFACT_FILENAME', defaultValue: 'my-app-1.0.0.tar.gz', description: 'Local filename for the downloaded artifact (e.g., my-app-1.0.0.tar.gz). This will also be the name on the remote server temporarily.')
        string(name: 'TARGET_SERVER_HOST', defaultValue: 'prod-server-01.example.com', description: 'Hostname or IP address of the production server')
        string(name: 'TARGET_SERVER_USER', defaultValue: 'deployuser', description: 'Username for SSH access to the production server')
        credentials(name: 'TARGET_SERVER_CREDENTIALS_ID', credentialType: 'SSH Username with private key', required: true, description: 'Jenkins Credentials ID (SSH Username with private key) to access the production server')
        string(name: 'REMOTE_BASE_PATH', defaultValue: '/opt/myapp', description: 'Base path on the remote server where the application will be deployed (e.g., /opt/myapp)')
        string(name: 'APP_SERVICE_NAME', defaultValue: 'myapp', description: 'Name of the service to manage (e.g., systemd service name like "myapp.service"). Leave empty if no service management is needed.')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Prepare Workspace') {
            steps {
                script {
                    echo "Preparing Jenkins workspace..."
                    sh "rm -rf ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}" // Clean up any previous downloads
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Download Artifact') {
            steps {
                script {
                    echo "Downloading artifact from ${params.ARTIFACT_URL} to ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}..."
                    // Use curl with -L to follow redirects and -o to specify output filename
                    // This command handles downloads from S3, Artifactory, or any HTTP/HTTPS source.
                    sh "curl -L -o ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} ${params.ARTIFACT_URL}"
                    sh "ls -lh ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            steps {
                script {
                    // Extract common extension from artifact filename for cleaner release directory naming
                    def releaseDirName = params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME.replaceFirst(/\.(tar\.gz|tgz|zip|jar)$/, '')
                    def remoteReleasePath = "${params.REMOTE_BASE_PATH}/releases/${releaseDirName}"
                    def remoteCurrentLink = "${params.REMOTE_BASE_PATH}/current"
                    def artifactExtension = ''
                    def matcher = params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME =~ /\.(tar\.gz|tgz|zip|jar)$/
                    if (matcher) {
                        artifactExtension = matcher[0][1] // Get the matched extension
                    } else {
                        // Fallback: simple dot extension or no extension
                        matcher = params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME =~ /\.[^\.]*$/
                        if (matcher) {
                            artifactExtension = matcher[0][0].substring(1) // Remove leading dot
                        }
                    }

                    echo "Deploying ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} to ${params.TARGET_SERVER_HOST}:${remoteReleasePath}"

                    // Use ssh-agent for secure SSH key management
                    sshagent(credentials: [params.TARGET_SERVER_CREDENTIALS_ID]) {
                        // Copy artifact to a temporary location on the remote server
                        sh """
                            echo "Copying artifact to /tmp/${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} on ${params.TARGET_SERVER_HOST}..."
                            scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \\
                                ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} \\
                                ${params.TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${params.TARGET_SERVER_HOST}:/tmp/${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}
                        """

                        // Execute deployment commands on the remote server
                        sh """
                            echo "Executing remote deployment commands on ${params.TARGET_SERVER_HOST}..."
                            ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \\
                                ${params.TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${params.TARGET_SERVER_HOST} \\
                                << 'EOF'
                                set -euo pipefail

                                # Create base directories if they don't exist
                                mkdir -p ${params.REMOTE_BASE_PATH}/releases
                                mkdir -p ${params.REMOTE_BASE_PATH}/shared # Common for logs, configs, etc.

                                # Create the specific release directory
                                mkdir -p ${remoteReleasePath}

                                # Move artifact from /tmp and extract/process it
                                echo "Moving artifact to ${remoteReleasePath}/ and processing..."
                                mv /tmp/${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} ${remoteReleasePath}/
                                cd ${remoteReleasePath}

                                if [[ "${artifactExtension}" == "tar.gz" || "${artifactExtension}" == "tgz" ]]; then
                                    echo "Extracting tar.gz archive..."
                                    tar -xzf ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}
                                elif [[ "${artifactExtension}" == "zip" ]]; then
                                    echo "Extracting zip archive..."
                                    unzip -q ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME} # -q for quiet
                                elif [[ "${artifactExtension}" == "jar" ]]; then
                                    echo "JAR file detected. Assuming it's a runnable JAR, no extraction."
                                    # For JARs, typically it's run directly. The 'current' symlink will point to this directory.
                                else
                                    echo "Unsupported or unrecognized artifact type: ${artifactExtension}. Assuming it's a single file, no extraction needed."
                                fi

                                # Remove the original artifact after processing (optional)
                                rm -f ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}

                                # Service management
                                if [ -n "${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME}" ]; then
                                    # Stop the service
                                    if systemctl is-active --quiet ${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME}; then
                                        echo "Stopping ${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME} service..."
                                        sudo systemctl stop ${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME}
                                    else
                                        echo "${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME} service is not active, skipping stop."
                                    fi
                                fi

                                # Update symbolic link to the new release
                                echo "Updating symbolic link from ${remoteCurrentLink} to ${remoteReleasePath}"
                                rm -f ${remoteCurrentLink}
                                ln -s ${remoteReleasePath} ${remoteCurrentLink}

                                # Service management
                                if [ -n "${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME}" ]; then
                                    # Start the service
                                    echo "Starting ${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME} service..."
                                    sudo systemctl start ${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME}
                                    sudo systemctl status ${params.APP_SERVICE_NAME} || true # Show status, don't fail if status command exits non-zero
                                fi

                                echo "Deployment complete for ${releaseDirName}."

                                # Optional: Clean up old releases, keep last 5
                                echo "Cleaning up old releases, keeping the 5 most recent..."
                                pushd ${params.REMOTE_BASE_PATH}/releases || exit 1
                                    ls -td * | tail -n +5 | xargs -r rm -rf
                                popd
                                EOF
                        """
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            script {
                echo "Cleaning up local workspace artifact: ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}"
                sh "rm -f ${params.ARTIFACT_FILENAME}"
            }
        }
        success {
            echo "Pipeline finished successfully!"
        }
        failure {
            echo "Pipeline failed!"
        }
    }
}
